category_title: Students with a history of school failure and resistance to traditional academic environments
minimal_context: The student is sitting in the electricity section classroom and is asked to read a textbook chapter and complete a worksheet on electrical theory.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please read this textbook chapter on electrical theory and complete the worksheet questions at your desk.

STUDENT: I do not like studying.

TUTOR: Just try to read the first page and answer the questions.

STUDENT: I don't have study habits for this kind of thing. I always missed classes anyway.

TUTOR: You need to finish the assigned work before the end of the period.

STUDENT: (Pushes the worksheet away) It was easy, but I was not interested in it. I'm not doing this if it isn't actually useful for anything.

options.A: Use a simple checklist to document the student's pragmatic language and communication skills during different classroom situations and assigned electrical theory activities throughout the term.
options.B: Conduct functional behavior analyses to target the purported variables of the student's behavior, and subject these specific variables to data-based hypothesis testing throughout the term.
options.C: Use a globalizing, interdisciplinary teaching methodology based on the student's specific interests, ensuring the entire learning process revolves around what is practically important to them.
options.D: Adhere to an established set of rules and penalties regarding classroom behavior, starting with a warning, followed by a 10-minute timeout, and then increasingly serious disciplinary measures.
